platform.review: AGREE. adidas.ie uses a custom platform likely built on SAP Commerce Cloud. The URL pattern /{slug}/{code}.html, ProductGroup JSON-LD, and internal /api/products/{code} endpoint are consistent with SAP CC.

anti_bot.review: AGREE. Confirmed by probe: direct HTTP blocked, standard Playwright blocked. Only UC Chrome (uc_chrome_none) accesses the site. This is Akamai Bot Manager as used by adidas globally.

scraping_mechanism.review: AGREE. UC Chrome is the only method that works. Direct HTTP and standard Playwright are both blocked. seleniumbase_uc is the correct strategy.

connectivity.review: CONFIRMED. Probe returned same results: uc_chrome_none, proxy_tier=none, js_rendering_needed=true.

jsonld.review: CONFIRMED. Block 1: OnlineStore (name, url, logo, contactPoint, hasMerchantReturnPolicy). Block 2: ProductGroup (@id, productGroupID, name, description, brand, aggregateRating, review, variesBy, hasVariant). Both have offers=EMPTY, confirming price is NOT in JSON-LD.

css_selectors.review: CONFIRMED. All 12 common selectors tested by probe returned NOT FOUND. The page is fully JS-rendered with React components using dynamically generated class names. No stable CSS selectors available in server-rendered HTML.

extraction_strategy.site_analyzer_said: Primary: internal API /api/products/{code}?fields=FULL. Fallback: JSON-LD + CSS.
extraction_strategy.review: AGREE with priority. The internal API returns ~68KB of structured product data including price, availability, variants, images. JSON-LD provides name, description, brand, ratings but NO price. CSS selectors are unreliable due to dynamic rendering. RECOMMENDED: Primary extraction via internal API (requires UC Chrome session), supplemented by JSON-LD for fields not in API.

connectivity.notes: UC Chrome (undetected Chrome) without proxy is the only method that works. Direct HTTP and standard Playwright are both blocked by Akamai. No proxy escalation needed.

extraction_methods.api_notes: Returns ~68KB structured JSON. Requires UC Chrome session cookies to access. Product code extracted from URL (e.g., JI0079 from /superstar-ii-shoes/JI0079.html). Contains price, availability, images, variants, descriptions, and all product metadata.
extraction_methods.js_dom_notes: Page is fully JS-rendered with React. All visible content (title, price, availability, images) requires JS execution. No server-rendered HTML content available for common selectors.

url.notes: Set by scraper from the input URL.

status_code.notes: Set by scraper from HTTP response.

scraped_at.notes: Set by scraper as ISO-8601 timestamp.

remarks.notes: Set by scraper for any warnings or notes.

title.notes: JSON-LD ProductGroup contains product name. Page title also confirmed: 'adidas Superstar II shoes - Black | adidas Ireland'. h1 NOT FOUND by probe.

price.notes: JSON-LD offers is EMPTY - price not in structured data. All price CSS selectors NOT FOUND by probe. Price is JS-rendered and must come from either the internal API or DOM scraping via UC Chrome session. The API is the reliable source.

original_price.notes: Not in JSON-LD (offers empty). Not in common CSS selectors. Must extract from API response if product is on sale.

currency.notes: adidas.ie is the Irish storefront. Currency is EUR. Should also be available in API response price.currencyIso or priceData.currency field.

availability.notes: JSON-LD offers empty (no availability). Common CSS selectors NOT FOUND. Must come from API response stock data.

description.notes: Available in JSON-LD ProductGroup.description. May be truncated; API may have full description.

sku.notes: ProductGroupID in JSON-LD likely contains the product code. API has explicit code field.

rating.notes: Available in JSON-LD ProductGroup.aggregateRating.ratingValue and ratingCount.

images.notes: Not in JSON-LD (ProductGroup has no image field). Must extract from API response images array.

variants.notes: JSON-LD hasVariant contains references to variant URLs. Full variant data (sizes, colors, prices, stock) is in the API response variants array. variesBy field in JSON-LD indicates what varies (e.g., size, color).

category.notes: Not in JSON-LD. Available in API response categories array.

location.notes: adidas.ie = Ireland storefront. Could also extract from API if available.

block_2.offers: EMPTY - no price or availability in JSON-LD

api_extraction.notes: Requires UC Chrome session cookies to bypass Akamai. The product code is extracted from URL path: e.g., JI0079 from /superstar-ii-shoes/JI0079.html. Returns ~68KB of structured JSON with complete product data including price, stock, images, variants, categories, and all metadata.

variants.jsonld_variesBy: Indicates what attributes vary (size, color, material)
variants.notes: The forceSelSize=11 query param in the test URL confirms size variants exist. Variant-specific pricing and availability may vary. Full variant data requires the API response.

page_structure.title: adidas Superstar II shoes - Black | adidas Ireland
page_structure.server_rendered_content: Minimal - only JSON-LD scripts and app shell
page_structure.client_rendered_content: All product UI (title, price, images, size selectors, add to cart)
page_structure.css_framework: Custom adidas design system with dynamically generated class names

extraction_flow: 2. Navigate to product page URL (let Akamai challenge resolve)
3. Wait for page to fully render (React hydration)
4. Extract JSON-LD ProductGroup block for: name, description, brand, rating, review_count, productGroupID
5. Extract product code from URL using regex /([A-Z0-9]+)\.html
6. Fetch internal API /api/products/{code}?fields=FULL using same browser session (session cookies included)
7. Parse API JSON response for: price, original_price, currency, availability, sku, images, variants, categories
8. Combine JSON-LD + API data into unified product record
9. Add derived fields: url, src_url, status_code, scraped_at, remarks
scraping_recommendation.retry_logic: If Akamai challenge appears, wait and retry. UC Chrome handles most challenges automatically.
